notes: Le Lectier Pear, Bergamot, Lemon, Kobushi Magnolia, Jasmine Sambac, Damask Rose, Tuberose, Musk, Amber, Sandalwood, Vetiver, Patchouli
style: Floral, Floral - Fruity, Floral - White, Fresh - Aquatic, Woody - Fresh
description: J-Scent

name: Cafe
notes: Coffee, Orange, Lemon, Orange Flower, Muguet, Violet, Vanilla, Musk, Cedarwood
description: J-Scent

name: Tender Peach
notes: Green Note, Lemon, Lime, Peach, Tuberose, Rose, Musk, Sandalwood, Raspberry
description: L'Artisan Parfumeur

name: Amber Chocolate
notes: Chocolate, Tonka Bean, Vanilla
style: Boozy, Gourmand, Natural/Organic, Woody - Dark
description: Amber Chocolate is a lush gourmand amber that should come with a sticker that reads âfor grown-ups onlyâ. Put aside any thoughts you might have of the kind of yummy vanilla-and-berries smell surrounding young girls like a nuclear cloud â this is seriously dark and sexy stuff. A molten dark chocolate note gets buffed out into an almondy haze of tonka bean, hints of hay and roast hazelnut flirting with the cacao to give it a slightly bitter, non-edible edge that sets it apart from other gourmands. The fact that this delicious scent is all-natural and artisanal means that itâs a guilt-free indulgence.

name: Hindu Kush
notes: Spice, Woody Notes, Incense, Ginger, Caraway, Nutmeg, Pepper
style: Green / Herbaceous, Natural/Organic, Resinous / Balsamic, Spicy, Woody - Spicy
description: Hindu Kush takes its name and inspiration from the 500-mile mountain range running along the hinterlands of Afghanistan, Kashmir, and Pakistan, characterized by wild pine forests that give way to jagged mountain peaks. The scent mirrors the sensory contrast between forest and stone to perfection, capturing in particular the chilly isolation of rugged places unreachable to most humans. It is a meditative scent that taps into our need for silence and contemplation.

name: Palermo, Don Corleone
notes: Vanilla, Tuberose, Tobacco
style: Gourmand
description: Palermo Don Corleone is a fragrance that represents the Sicilian people, a perfume in which everything is exaggerated, starting with an exaggeration of Vanilla. The love for this aroma is of course in line with the Sicilian passion for pastries, sweets and chocolates, but it also denotes a very strong sentimental attachment to the mother, the need to be comforted and of tenderness. I wanted a fragrance that would be extra-large also in sensuality, but having in it the hidden dark dangerous side of Sicily. To obtain this result I played Vanilla in an accord with Tuberose and Tobacco.

name: Holy Water
notes: Frankincense, Sandalwood, Orange, Orange Blossom, Rose
style: Floral - White, Fresh - Clean, Natural/Organic, Skin Scent / Minimal
description: A rose-and-sandal attar in the body of a French eau de cologne. In the Middle East and India, some attars are composed specifically to protect the soul from djinns, or evil spirits. In the West, we use citrusy colognes to refresh the body in hot weather. Both concepts are rooted in the belief that certain materials in perfumery possess an excoriating, cleansing power to drive out bad spirits and bad smells. Abdes Salaam Attarâs Holy Water (Aqua Santa) is a perfume  bridge between the Eastern concept of purifying oneâs soul and the Western concept of refreshing the physical. In pairing a bitter orange note with a dusty, ethereal frankincense, and placing it all in the context of a fresh eau de cologne, Holy Water emerges as a true hybrid between the Eastern and Western âeaux de protectionâ.

name: Mecca Balsam
notes: Indian Tuberose, Tobacco, Rose, Benzoin, Agarwood, Incense, Labdanum, Tonka Bean
style: Natural/Organic
description: Opening on a rich, smoky stew of incense over the crackle of dried tobacco leaf, Mecca Balsam sends the senses reeling, as if youâd suddenly stepped into the scent trail of a thousand pilgrims holding burning censers aloft as they circle the Black Stone in Mecca. Despite the tarry heft of the sticky balsams and resins, there is an airy sweetness to the underlying structure â a sort of smoked-out incense fairy dust â that elevates the scent and keeps it airborne.

name: Milano Caffe
notes: Coffee, Cappuccino, Chocolate, Iris, Woody Notes, Spicy Notes, Opoponax, Tonka Bean, Amber
style: Natural/Organic
description: Milan is a city that prides itself on the uncompromising purity of its espresso, and in Milano CaffÃ©, the roasted bitterness of the bean meets its match in a square of viciously dark chocolate. If youâve ever sipped an espresso while nibbling on a bar of 80% cacao chocolate, then youâll be familiar with the dark, burnt edge that connects them on the Venn diagram of taste. Dubrana feeds it to us straight for a while â no sugar or milk â and itâs so intense that little sips are in order. Earthy and bold, the scentâs velvety thickness wraps around us like the steamy vapors in a Milanese coffee house.

name: Oakmoss
notes: Vetiver, Sandalwood, Oakmoss
style: Earthy, Green / Herbaceous, Natural/Organic, Salty / Oceanic, Woody - Spicy
description: Oakmoss (Quercia di Muschio) is deceptively named, because, despite the clear presence of oakmoss, this scent is far more about vetiver than moss. Still, the genius of the fragrance â for us â lies in the fact that Dominique Dubrana has obviously mined the areas of convergence between these two materials to emerge with a creature that has a foot in both worlds. Thus, the marine-like inkiness of the oakmoss connects with the rooty earthiness of vetiver via the crunch of sea salt and a shadowy âforest floorâ gloom. In the background, a dry, minimally smoky sandalwood provides a palette against which the notes of earth, grass, root, and lichen can really pop.

name: Tabac
notes: Tobacco, Vanilla, Tonka Bean, Labdanum, Spicy Notes, Resins
style: Earthy, Natural/Organic, Resinous / Balsamic, Smoky, Woody - Dark, Woody - Spicy
description: Tobacco fragrances often get the âweightâ thing wrong. Too much amber or vanilla, and it sinks under the heft of its own richness, but too light and it feels like a sheer wash of ash on the skin. Tabac by Abdes Salaam Attar gets it right by pairing a bold tobacco leaf with a sheer dried-fruit amber and a bitter, earthy vanilla - elements that serve to modulate each otherâs behavior and sidestep any excessive sweetness.

name: Oud Caravan No. 3
notes: Natural Aged Wild Bengali Oud Oil, Dates, Labdanum
style: Animalic, Natural/Organic, Resinous / Balsamic, Smoky, Woody - Dark
description: Abdes Salaam Attarâs Oud Caravan series is the worldâs first (and probably only) crowd-sourced oud perfume collection. Commissioned by an Arab customer to compose an oud perfume for the Gulf region, Dominique Dubrana decided to make it an open source project, enlisting the help of a group of oud connoisseurs on Basenotes.com to stress-test his offering. Oud Caravan No. 1 was originally intended to be the sole perfume in the endeavor â what Dubrana termed âthe Ultimate Oud Fragranceâ â but like a really good conversation with a circle of friends, nobody wanted it to end. Riffing off each other in endlessly creative ways, Dubrana harnessed the energy of the group to create the first, then second, and finally third Oud Caravan. Today, all three Oud Caravan perfumes fit together thematically and sequentially as a cohesive statement on the mysterious material that is oud oil.

name: Cuoio Dei Dolci
notes: Castoreum, Tonka, Tobacco, Cocoa, Mandarin, Mysore Sandalwood, Ylang Ylang, Vanilla.
style: Boozy, Creamy, Resinous / Balsamic, Smoky, Sweet, Woody - Spicy
description: La Via Del Profumo

name: Functional Fragrance
notes: Green Cardamom, Iris, Cilantro, Palo Santo
description: Cognitive function and the olfactory system are closely linked and smells can trigger both emotional and physiological responses. Put simply: scent is one of the fastest ways to alter your emotional state. Functional Fragrance is, well, a fragrance with a function, designed to help you reset at times of high stress.

name: Forest Lungs
notes: Woody, Smoky, Tart Citrus
description: Using science to work with nature, Forest Lungs is a sensory supplement designed to support the reduction of everyday stress. Inspired by the concept of âmedicalizing natureâ, it fuses olfactory chemistry and patented technology to replicate the molecular compounds produced in forests called phytoncides. Phytoncides are credited for the positive health benefits associated with forest bathing, namely stress reduction, anxiety reduction and boosting the parasympathetic (rest) nervous system. Bring the outside in with Forest Lungs.

name: Mind Energy
notes: Clary Sage, Pink Peppercorn, Geranium, Clove, Juniper, Cedarwood
style: Earthy, Floral - Citrus, Green / Herbaceous
description: The Nue Co.

name: Water Therapy
notes: Seaweed, Salt, Cardamom, Rose
description: We all have a biological connection to water. Studies show the sight, sound and feeling of water have a positive effect on our mental and physical health, from producing mood-boosting neurochemicals to enhancing immune function.

name: No. 10 Desir du Coeur
notes: Amber, Musc, Aromatics
style: Woody - Dark, Woody - Spicy
description: Unravel the desires of the heart. A modern, artistic outlook on fragrance, unlike traditional mixing techniques. The combination of captives and molecules creates a unique and distinguishable scent specific to the individual.

name: No. 4 Candy
notes: Red Fruits, Cherry, Raspberry Blossom, Tiare Flower, Vanilla, Cotton Candy, Caramel
description: The original Thomas Kosmala No. 4 AprÃ¨s lâAmour was an immediate breakout hit- a sheer, warm musk with a sultry amber sensuality and stunning longevity that elevated it far beyond "skin scent" and into secret weapon territory, it quickly became a cult favorite, as well as a top choice for potent, sophisticated layering. In an inspiring dialogue between perfumer and audience, Thomas Kosmala now presents a set of dynamic new reimagined versions of No. 4, layering vibrant new themes over the seductive intensity and projection of the original.

name: No. 4 Apres l'Amour
notes: Bitter Orange, Lemon Zest, Aromatic Notes, Fresh Spice, Dry Wood, Amber, Musk
style: Fresh - Citrus, Powdery / Soft, Skin Scent / Minimal, Spicy
description: AprÃ¨s lâAmour is a 1960âs Serge Gainsbourg song filtered through the synth pop sensibilities of the 1980âs: candied lemon-rind musks and animalic notes suspended in a haze of modern woody-ambery aromachemicals. It smells like human skin after lovemaking â erotically charged, moist, musky â but thanks to a mesh of powerfully dry, smoky woods and electric ambers, broadcast at high volume to the rest of the world.

name: No. 7 Le Sel de la Terre
notes: Bergamot, Italian Lemon, Fresh Marine Notes, Aqua, Fresh Floral, Fresh Wood, Musk
style: Fresh - Aquatic, Fresh - Citrus, Fresh - Clean, Salty / Oceanic, Skin Scent / Minimal
description: Le Sel de la Terre is proof that Leonardo da Vinciâs assertion that âsimplicity is the ultimate sophisticationâ is correct. Infuse a cloudy white musk with fresh lemon and crusty sea salt notes, and what you have is utter perfection. If it sounds trite or you think itâs been done to death, then know that Kosmalaâs take distinguishes itself from the pack simply by virtue of smelling entirely natural, even when it is (clearly) not.

name: No. 8 Tonic Vert
notes: Bergamot, Lemon, Mandarin, Lime, Mint, Geranium, Pomelo, Vetiver, Musk, Oakmoss
style: Earthy, Fresh - Citrus, Green / Herbaceous
description: Tonic Vert is a true tonic for the winter blues. With its crushed mint and sour citrus topnotes, the scent starts out with the stinging clarity of early morning sunshine cutting across a dark room. Unlike other green fragrances, it is neither soapy-soft or sappy-juicy. Rather, it is dry, dry, dry throughout, an essential bitterness focusing the mind and straightening the spine.

name: No. 9 Bukhoor
notes: Woody Accords, Smoky Accords, Agarwood, Amber, Musk
style: Resinous / Balsamic, Smoky, Spicy, Woody - Dark, Woody - Spicy
description: Bukhoor, or bakhoor as it is sometimes known, is the Arabic name for woodchips that have been soaked in precious essential oils, mixed with shards of frankincense and other resins, and finally pressed into soft, brownish bricks to be smoked slowly over an incense burner. All over the Middle East, bukhoor is burned generously to banish domestic smells of cooking and to make the home smell warm and welcoming to visitors.

name: Awake
notes: Balkans Santos Coffee, Green Cardamom, Italian Lemon, Haitian Vetiver
style: Creamy, Earthy, Gourmand, Spicy, Woody - Light
description: The opening jolts you awake, like a loved one passing a freshly brewed espresso in front of your nose as you begin to nod off. Hot, black, intense roasted bean with the burnt edge of wood crackling in the grate. The green bite of cardamom giving it a tart edge. Awakeâs darkness is tantalizingly profound, but the perfumer has thoughtfully slipped a nugget of toasted hazelnut in with the coffee to buffer it from any sharpness or acridity.

name: Malt
notes: Whiskey, Rum, Leather, Seaweed, Patchouli.
style: Boozy, Fresh - Aquatic, Sweet
description: What happens when you cross a peaty Laphroaig whiskey with an aquatic? Malt. Sure, itâs the answer to a question we donât remember asking, but weâre so happy that such a thing actually exists. Opening with a gorgeously caramelized whiskey note that conjures up both the smokiness and the toffee-like depth of an aged whiskey from the highlands of Scotland, Malt quickly slips into a firmly mid-tone register: rich but light, with toasty brown woods unobtrusively holding it together.

name: Dark
notes: Cocoa, Dark Chocolate, Cinnamon, Hazelnut, Vanilla
style: Creamy, Gourmand, Sweet
description: Oh man, this is good. A thick pour of molten dark chocolate to begin with, as strong and as intense as an espresso, but absent any of the metallic or animalic aspects that can sometimes emerge in dark chocolate. This is dark chocolate idealized. Itâs not sweet or creamy, but thereâs a strong hint of caramelized milk running underneath the rafters of the scent to pull it back from acridity. Itâs pure unadulterated dark chocolate bliss.

name: Night
notes: Bulgarian Rose, Cumin, Saffron, Agaroud
style: Earthy, Floral - Ambery Spice, Woody - Light
description: Night will make you blush. A finger strokeâs worth of cumin imbues an innocently rosy musk with enough inner thigh naughtiness to stop anyone in their trackâs, retrace their steps, and follow that divine scent to its source: you. Night is sexy because it dances on that barely perceptible line between clean and dirty, never tipping its hand too far in one direction or the other, and therefore keeping you guessing all the way through.

name: Smoke
notes: Balkans Tobacco Leaves, Nicotine, Cade, Benjoin, Tonka Bean
style: Earthy, Smoky, Woody - Dark
description: Smoking. The ultimate modern taboo. A dirty word. And yet, for those old enough to remember, there is something viscerally pleasing about the acrid aroma of a freshly-lit cigarette â perhaps because all the more attractive for its illicit, banned-in-public places, worse-than-marijuana notoriety it now enjoys.

name: Haze
notes: Absinthe, Armoise, Eucalyptus, Spearmint, Clary Sage
style: Exotic, Green / Herbaceous, Woody - Fresh
description: Haze is the aromatic fog of a Bohemian cafÃ©, where the lurid green fairy of absinthe mingles with the sage-bush dryness of an illicit spliff. But surprisingly, before it is all this, it is a whirlwind brush with a kitchen garden after a downpour, a riotous vortex of crushed mint, snapped plant stems, and the herbal dewiness of clary sage. It smells like fresh lavender buds and wet soil, a winning mixture of sun-warmed herbs and the cool dampness of a spring shower.

name: Akro Discovery Set
description: Ten transgressive fragrances: Caffeine. Chocolate. Cupcakes. Summer. Tobacco. Party. Rose Macarons. Happiness. Tea. Nature.

name: Ink
notes: Vetiver, Tar, Black Ink, Jasmine, Birch
style: Animalic, Earthy, Resinous / Balsamic, Smoky, Spicy, Woody - Dark
description: Everyone remembers their first tattoo; the fear, the sensation, the adrenaline rush. For those whoâve fallen under the spell of this ancient art, itâs much more than a bit of body decoration; itâs a reminder of a time, a place- a fleeting moment that becomes a permanent memento.

name: Bake
notes: Lemon Peel, Rum, Chantilly Cream, Praline, Brown Sugar, Vanilla
style: Gourmand
description: One of the aromas of modern life we find most alluring is that of bakeries and cake shops, the scent of fresh pastry and sticky sugar brightening up the most humdrum of days. One of Akro's favorite bakeries in all of London is Carnaby Streetâs Crumbs & Dollies, and inspired by their famous lemon cupcakes, Akro created a new perfume: BAKE.

name: Rise
notes: Coconut Water, Pina Colada, Toffee, Tiare Flower, Ambroxan
style: Fruity, Tropical / Beachy
description: Itâs that of year when the days are getting shorter, the nights are getting longer and you just wish summer could have lasted forever. At Akro, we canât promise to keep the weather good, but we can bring you a little hit of summer whenever you want.

name: B683
notes: Black Pepper, Saffron, Chili Pepper, Nutmeg, Violet Leaf, Amber, Cistus Absolute, Musk, Patchouli, Santal Wood, Oakmoss, Ambroxan
style: Earthy, Resinous / Balsamic, Woody - Spicy
description: âI like the raw and yet so chic aspect of this perfume: a very refined and wild fragrance.â â Quentin Bisch, perfumer

name: Ganymede
notes: Mandarin, Saffron, Violet, Osmanthus Absolute, Akigalawood, Immortelle Absolute
style: Salty / Oceanic, Woody - Fresh, Woody - Light
description: Ganymede, the rocky satellite of Jupiter discovered in 1610 by Galileo, both luminous and covered with salt-water oceans. In collaboration with Quentin Bisch, Marc-Antoine Barrois has dreamt of the essence of this new planet: an elegant and unexpected harmony around a quartet of mandarin, violet, immortelle and suede.

name: B683 Extrait
notes: Pepper Berries Essence, Cumin Seeds Essence, Green Apple Accord, Safraleine, Absolute Violet Leaf, Oud, Patchouli Heart Indonesia Orpur, Sandalwood Album Essence Australian Orpur, Vanilla
description: The most carnal expression of B683. Long time fragrance collaborators Marc-Antoine Barrois and Quentin Bisch are back at it again offering us a new magnificent addition to the house. B683 Extrait advances the original's intention. A sensual and captivating leather to be worn as a second skin with an intense and remarkable trail. With this reinterpretation, the two creators offer a deep dive into the most captivating character of B683, an immersion into the heart of this perfume and its signature: the warmth and depth of a woody-leathery accord. In its extrait form, its intensity surges to become an authentic âpatchouli leather.â Building on that incredible base, the other notes come in to shape its form with tactile sensuality. Pepper and cumin vibrate throughout its composition, oud wood enhance the accordâs density while the vanilla brightens it with a caressing roundness. Charismatic, Sensual, Enigmatic. This olfactive creation invites you to discover the universe of these couturier and their vision of timeless elegance.

name: Encelade
notes: Rhubarb, Vetiver, Atlas Cedar, Tonka Bean, Australian Sandalwood
style: Woody - Dark, Woody - Spicy
description: Marc-Antoine Barrois presents a new planet, a new universe.  A fresh jungle on the side of a volcano, Encelade is a powerful and captivating scent.  Sensual and unique, it's green and woody notes are meant to seduce men and women alike.

name: Ganymede Extrait
notes: Everlasting Flower, Incense, Myrrh, Safraleine, Akigalawood, Mandarin
style: Woody - Amber, Woody - Spicy
description: Ganymede, this world is so loaded with aromatics that it becomes combustible, its sky is gleaming with mandarin and holy fumaroles of incense and myrrh. It's a planet with warm dunes where spicy and caramelized everlasting flower rushes. Fiery heart of this fragrance, it reminds that gods gave Ganymede eternal life. Is it a burning skin hidden under the wild leather? World perfume or carnal one, this precious extract of perfume deploys in it is dizzying trail the seduction of a great classic.

name: For Him Eau de Toilette
notes: Lemon, Bergamot, Petitgrain, Lavender, Rosemary, Basil, Clary Sage, Sandalwood, Patchouli, Clove, Cinnamon, Amber, Musk
style: Classic Masculine, Cologne, Fresh - Citrus, Green / Herbaceous, Woody - Fresh
description: A bona fide 1980's cult classic, Milano Cento was born in an era of cultural futurism and modernist design, but with an eye towards the classics. Now, nearly 40 years later, it's never been more compelling.

name: Rose de Jamal
notes: Moroccan Damascene Rose Absolute, Moroccan Cedarwood, Madagascan Pink Pepper C02, Morocan Naneh Mint, Lavender
style: Exotic, Floral - Ambery Spice, Fougere
description: Rose de Jamal asks you to rethink this iconic perfume note, long consigned in the mainstream perfume imagination as something stodgy, old fashioned, and too conventionally feminine. And one whiff of it will have you feeling, to borrow a song lyric, like a perfume virgin, sniffing rose as iffor the very first time.

name: Chypre Azural
notes: Sicilian Tarocco Orange Oil, Egyptian Centifolia Rose Absolute Grand Cru, Indonesian Patchouli, Ambergris Tincture, Tarragon From The Alps
style: Chypre, Fresh - Citrus
description: If youâve long thought of the chypre category as dark and mysterious, think again. Chypre Azural is as juicy and fresh as the Sicilian Tarocco orange at its heart. Typically a contrast between fresh top notes and a mossy accord, that characteristic contrast can go in many directions, and in Chypre Azural it tips toward aromatic and bright, with patchouli grounding it in that family.

name: Musc des Sables
notes: Italian Green Mandarin, Indonesian Patchouli Grand Cru, Ambergris Tincture, Orris Absolute Grand Cru, Benzoin, Cardamom, Cinnamon, Musk
style: Chypre, Floral - Spicy
description: What do you get when you combine two of the costliest ingredients in perfumeryâambergris and orrisâwith animalic musks and spices? In Antoine Lieâs hands, a mysteriously sexy elixir that melts into skin, leaving a sheer but substantial trace of fairy dust thatâs subtly sweet, spicy, sensuous, and ever-evolving.

name: Iris Perle
notes: French Orris Absolute Grand Cru, Morrocan Mimosa Absolute, Indian Jasmine Absolute, Madagascan Ylang-ylang, Clary Sage From The Alps
style: Floral - Light, Floral - White
description: Like some beautiful faces so gorgeous youâre not sure what feature to pay attention to first, the elegant Iris Perle is an embarrassment of riches.

name: Les Indemodables - Discovery Set
description: Artisanal, transparent, authentic, luxury, exclusive.These arenât just buzzwords to the founders of French brand Les IndÃ©modablesâwhich means âtimeless classics that never go out of styleââ but rather inform every aspect of their stunning perfumes.

name: Cuir de Chine
notes: Chinese Osmanthus Absolute 1%, Chinese Osmanthus âalcoolatâ Grand Cru 10%, Turkish âtabac Blondâ Absolute .2%, Alpine Clary Sage
style: Exotic, Floral - Ambery Spice
description: In Les IndÃ©modablesâ Cuir de Chine (âChinese Leatherâ), the usually delicate, apricot-faceted osmanthus note is boldly paired with a beautifully fresh and realistic tobacco leaf to create an accord.

name: Fougere Emeraude
notes: Indian Tuberose, French Lavender Grand Cru, Venezuelan Tonka Absolute, Moroccan Mimosa Absolute Grand Cru, Alpine Clary Sage
style: Fougere
description: A tuberose fougÃ¨re. Thatâs not something you hear about every day, but once you smell it in FougÃ¨re Emeraude, it seems like the perfect way to both highlight and steer this floral note in an interesting direction that deconstructs the gender categories a floral or a fougÃ¨re would ordinarily suggest.

name: Escale En Haiti
notes: Pink Pepper, Juniper, Haitian Vetiver, Tonka Bean, Myrhh
style: Earthy, Spicy, Woody - Spicy
description: Perhaps the most classic of all scents, the "L'eau de Cologne" style stays perpetually in fashion because of its wonderful simplicity. Featuring a lighter concentration that puts its emphasis on freshness and wearability, an EDC can actually be an ideal format to showcase exquisite natural materials in high concentrations that might feel overwhelming as part of a richer, more busy perfume. With Escale En Haiti, Les Indemodables transports us to the bustling and beautiful Les Cayes region of Haiti, and their signature crop- vetiver, considered by many to be the best in all the world.

name: Escale En Indonesie
notes: Bergamot, Lemon, Neroli, Indonesian Ambergris, Jasmine, Sandalwood
style: Floral - Citrus, Floral - White, Fresh - Citrus, Salty / Oceanic
description: Perhaps the most classic of all scents, the "L'eau de Cologne" style stays perpetually in fashion because of its wonderful simplicity. Featuring a lighter concentration that puts its emphasis on freshness and wearability, an EDC can actually be an ideal format to showcase exquisite natural materials in high concentrations that might feel overwhelming as part of a richer, more busy perfume.

description: Aesop

name: Ambre Khandjar
notes: Mandarin, Black Plum, Cistus Labdanum Aboslute, Vanilla Pod O2, Orris Absolute, Ylang Ylang, Sandalwood, Patchouli, Benzoin Resinoid
description: Ex Idolo

name: Jardins de Misfah
notes: Cardamom, Nutmeg, Date Accord, Rose Absolute, Rose Essence, Saffron, Almond
description: In the village of Misfah Al Abreyeen, a cornucopia of pomegranates, peaches, figs, and apricots grow abundantly in the midst of the mountain aridity, with marvelous palm trees as far as the eye can see, their branches heavy with the weight of dates. It is the bounty of legend come to life, an ingeniously engineered garden paradise created by warriors who set down their weapons to grow instead of destroy.

name: Suma d'Orient
notes: Cypriol, Rum, Cocoa, Patchouli, Sandalwood, Tonka Bean, Cashmere Wood, Gaiac Wood, Clear Wood, Musk
description: Perris Monte Carlo

name: Mr. Vetiver
notes: Cardamom, Grapefruit, Lime, Basil, Tarragon, Geranium, Vetiver. Piri Piri, Amber Wood, Mosses
description: Widian

name: Fleur des Fleurs
notes: Bergamot, Saffron, Grapefruit, Jasmin, Ylang-Ylang, Tubereuse, Glycine, Benzoin Tears, Sandalwood, Vanilla, Amber, Oud Wood
description: Une Nuit Nomade

name: Murmure des Dieux
notes: Frangipani, Almond, Vanilla, Musks
style: Floral - Light
description: The Different Company

name: Bohemian Soul
notes: Artemisia Absinthium, Olibanum, Guaiac Wood, Iris, Myrrh, Musc, Sandalwood
description: In 1966 the documentary 'Endless Summer' was released, chronicling two American surfersâ quest for the perfect wave. From the end of their surfboards, we experience the desire to push time zones further away so the summer never ends. This quest continues, with the entire planet becoming a refuge of immense beaches punctuated with hiding places where free spirits love to converge.

name: Memory Motel
notes: Bergamot, Iris, Incense, Carnation, Mosses, Patchouli, Vanilla, Tobacco, Leather
description: When Andy Warhol moved to Montauk, he became an anchor point, a lighthouse for an entire generation of artists. He rented his mansion for a summer to the Rolling Stones, who were rehearsing for their tour and pre-recording what would be one of their greatest albums, âBlack and Blueâ, which included the melancholy ballad âMemory Motelâ.

name: Une Nuit Nomade Discovery Set
description: More than a collection of fragrances, Une Nuit Nomade presents us with a global expedition. A map of sorts to venture forth and celebrate the joys of exploration of foreign lands. Lose yourself in the reconnaissance and remember: it's not about the destination, it's about the journey.

name: Sugar Leather
notes: Cinnamon, Plum, Amyris, Leather, Caramel, Tonka Bean, Patchouli, Labdanum
style: Woody Ambery Floral
description: Sora Dora

name: Love at first sight
notes: Passion Fruit, Bergamot, Acacia Blossom, Ylang Ylang, Lilacs, Cashmeran, Ambroxan
description: Springtime is mild in Johannesburg. Jacaranda takes over the city with its mauve coat and sits majestically for a season. Its bell-shaped flowers sway in the wind, giving off a bewitching fragrance - a hymn to love. Eager acacias welcome this declaration with fluttering hearts and flushed cheeks.

name: Sucre Noir
notes: Orchid, Vanilla Pod, Sugar Cane
description: Sucre Noir deserves the same Holy Grail status granted by vanilla lovers to Tihota by Indult or Vaniglia by Mazzolari. One of the most luxurious vanilla scents weâve ever smelled, it is the olfactory equivalent of being wrapped up in the purest, âwhitestâ cashmere blanket that costs  more than most of us earn in a month.

name: Harem Soiree
notes: Ambergris, Rose, Myrrh
description: A boozy rose dipped in honey and plunged into the salty rawness of ambergris, Harem Soiree is a stunning attar-like fragrance that takes no prisoners in the spiced amber genre. The ambergris lends a fiercely saline, windswept edge to the ambery underpinnings of the fragrance, joining with a resinous, licorice-like myrrh to render a picture of an unsweet, unpolished chunk of amber resin that has just washed up on a beach somewhere along the Baltic coast.

name: Samharam
notes: Myrrh, Incense, Benzoin
description: Samharam is a rich incense scent that traces a straight line to the ancient aromas of myrrh and benzoin that would have accompanied the caravan traders that moved from across the deserts from the spice and resin markets of Somalia and Oman to Venice and beyond. Opening with a triumphantly coca cola-ish myrrh with smoky licorice overtones, Samharam rolls on like a stone, picking up the deeply sooty nuances of Somali frankincense and elemi, as well as the sweeter âhot brown paperâ powderiness of benzoin.

name: Bisquit
notes: Cocoa, Coffee, Chocolate, Vanilla, Bitter Orange
style: Gourmand, Sweet
description: 4160 Tuesdays

name: Oeilleres
notes: Eucalyptus, Broom, Chamomile, Lavender, Cumin, Incense, Hay, Musk, Styrax
style: Animalic, Earthy, Floral - Ambery Spice
description: âI want to echo the flowers I photograph without referring to any one in particular. Therefore, along with Marc-Antoine Corticchiato, we decided to exclude rose, jasmine and all the overly tangible flowers. We managed to express the general idea of floralcy through the dried flower, pollen and honey facets of chamomile and broom. To connect the scent with the figures I photograph, I also wanted to convey the feel of flesh. In order to achieve this, we brought out the animal aspects of vegetal notes, as well as various spices and resins.  Though it wasnât an end in itself, the fragrance is bursting with natural ingredients â sometimes in very high doses, all the better to express the organic duality of my images. Between the delicacy of a fading petal and the radicality of a fold of skin.â â Roberto Greco

name: Porter sa Peau
description: Wearing your skin is meant to reflect my thinking, however distorted it may be - and my experience. Wearing your skin like you wear blinders, to see better and farther, will have been my way of observing myself through this photographic series. So I had to bend down to perceive the shimmering image of another myself.

name: Fougere L'Aube
notes: Lavender, Oakmoss, Geranium, Citrus, Hay, Galbanum, Costus, Musk, Sandalwood, Camphor, Amber, Moroccan Rose
style: Fougere
description: Boy, Manuel Cross wasnât messing around when he made FougÃ¨re LâAube. Modelled after the first fougÃ¨re ever to be made, the original FougÃ¨re Royale by Houbigant, FougÃ¨re LâAube heralds a triumphant return to the unapologetically masculine roots of the fougÃ¨re concept â the fresh, bitter aroma created by the marriage of lavender, oakmoss, and labdanum, given aromatic interest by way of minty geranium leaf and the sting of camphor.

name: Chypre-Siam
notes: Oakmoss, Kaffir Lime, Civet, Jasmine, Ylang, Leather, Spices, Holy Basil, Sandalwood, Benzoin
style: Chypre
description: Chypre-Siam is a joyful middle finger to IFRA. Unrepentedly teeming with enough oakmoss to make an EU official faint, this is a fragrance that lays lie to the notion that they âjust donât make âem like they used to anymore.â With Chypre-Siam, Manuel Cross proves for once and for all that itâs not only possible make an authentic chypre in this day and age, but that itâs possible to do it so well that it sounds like a battle cry.

name: Tabac Vert
notes: Tobacco, Carnation, Oakmoss, Sandalwood, Amber, Pepper, Bergamot, Rose, Jasmine
style: Green / Herbaceous, Spicy
description: Tabac Vert is a gentle tobacco breeze blowing in from the past, when tobacco-focused fragrance compositions were not sweetened with vanilla or tonka bean. With references to vintage Tabarome and Krizia Moods, Tabac Vert presents us with a gentlemanly vision of what tobacco can smell like when played with a subtle hand.

name: Mousse Illuminee
notes: Treemoss, Frankincense, Green Cypress, Artemisia, Laurel, Cedar, White Floral Notes, White Musk
style: Green / Herbaceous
description: Mousse IlluminÃ©e is a delightful anachronism. It smells manly in the best possible way â soapy, bright, and almost cleansing in its bitterness, a cornucopia of camphoraceous green notes such as cypress, laurel, and artemisia stuffed into a cedar chest, aromatizing it from the inside out. FougÃ¨res used to smell like this, once upon a time, we remember. Now, shorn of any vanilla or sweetness, Mouse IlluminÃ©e returns the fougÃ¨re to its original form, stripping it back until we are left with a powerful and invigorating âforest fernâ accord that is unabashedly masculine.

name: Derviche
notes: Bergamot, Labdanum, Vanilla, Jasmine, Olibanum, Sandalwood, Musk, Tobacco Leaf, Leather, Saffron, Civet
description: The brief Manuel Cross set himself for Derviche was simple â honor the famously soft green, powdery vanilla of vintage Emeraude by Coty but give it the power of one of those ambers coming out of the Middle-Eastern houses. In the end, Derviche smells very little like Emeraude but somehow features the same idea, which is to say a perfect balance struck between the tartness of citrus and the creamy sweetness of amber.

name: Jasmin Antique
notes: Jasmine Grandiflorum Blossoms, Musk, Clove, Vanilla
style: Creamy, Floral - Spicy, Floral - White, Powdery / Soft
description: Rogue Perfumery

name: Flos Mortis
notes: Indolic Tuberose Absolute, Indian Jasmine, Osmanthus, Red Currant, Leather, Musk
style: Classic Feminine, Floral - White, Powdery / Soft
description: Flos Mortis (flower of death) seems a bit dark and droll for a perfume concept but there is nothing dead or dull about it. This hardcore tuberose fragrance is a narcotic floral composed to be an indolic dirge, headed by tuberose and Indian jasmine. The composition focuses on Tuberose's indolic properties from both the fresh blossoms and the absolute. The indole note in Flos Mortis actually makes up 5% of the formula but is tempered with some Indian jasmine (grandiflorum) which shares similar indolic properties. The addition of red currant and osmanthus add some smooth and delicious fruitiness, while the raw black leather leather accord carries forth the rest of the animalic onslaught.

name: Tuberose & Moss
notes: Tuberose, Oakmoss, Vanilla Cream, Allspice Berries, Cedar, Labdanum, Musk
style: Creamy, Floral - Fruity, Floral - White, Green / Herbaceous, Powdery / Soft
description: Rogue Perfumery

name: Champs Lunaires
notes: Tuberose, White Rose Petals, Pomelo, Sandalwood, Coconut Milk, Musk
description: An intangible summer night;Moonlit fields of white flowers.

name: Bon Monsieur
notes: Lavender, Bergamot, Fir Balsam, Oakmoss, Geranium, Carnation, Lily Of The Valley, Cedar, Patchouli, Sandalwood, Musk
description: A fragrance of pure nostalgia inspired by the powerhouse men's fragrances of the 1980s, Bon Monsieur is a classic men's scent with the unique power of Rogue's unparalleled ingredients. Drawing on the sophistication and potency of beloved scents like Tsar, Carlo Corinto and Duc de Vervins, this is a lavender rich fougere both instantly recognizable and also dynamically bright.

name: Derviche II
notes: Citrus, Mediterranean Herbs, Incense Woods, Sugared Fruits, Amber, Tobacco, Vanilla, Musk
style: Fruity, Resinous / Balsamic, Spicy, Sweet
description: Rogue Perfumery

name: Rogue Discovery Set
description: Runaway hit brand Rogue Perfumery is now offering a trial set of their best-selling fragrances.  Perfect for lovers of vintage-style scents, each set includes a 1.5ml rollerball sample of:

name: Rostracto
notes: Rose Absolute, Fir Balsam Absolute, Artemisia, Benzoin Resin, Charred Cedar, Musk
style: Classic Feminine, Floral, Powdery / Soft, Woody - Spicy
description: Villa Erbatium

name: Targhee Forest
notes: Juniper Berry, Fir Balsam, Sagebrush, Citrus, Pine, Oakmoss, Geranium, Amber, Cedarwood, Patchouli, Musk
style: Chypre, Fougere
description: While previous Rogue releases have been inspired by the glamour and grandeur of vintage perfumes and classical styles, Rogue perfumer Manuel Cross considers Targhee Forest to be his first "scent of place," an evocation of a specific location in time and space. That place is the vast, unspoiled beauty of the Caribou-Targhee National Forest, and from the first sniff, you can absolutely picture yourself welcoming the dawn among the towering lodgepole pines. The bracing morning freshness of wild sage and crisply aromatic juniper begin to slowly warm with the emergence of sappy fir balsam, balancing earthy vegetation and green oakmoss with sun-warmed, resinous woods. The effect is evocative and naturalistic, but with a certain compositional refinement that fans of Rogue will find satisfyingly sophisticated. Grounding, soothing, and elegant, Targhee Forest is as uniquely special as the wilderness that it takes its name from.

name: Radish Vetiver
notes: Radish, Cypriol, Guaiac, Vetiver, Akigalawood
style: Earthy, Fresh - Citrus, Green / Herbaceous, Woody - Fresh, Woody - Spicy
description: Comme des Garcons: Accident

name: Chlorophyll Gardenia
notes: Cosmofruit, Gardenia, Spearmint, Pink Pepper, Black Pepper, Galbanum, Pea Accord, Musk
description: Comme des Garcons: Accident

name: Celluloid Galbanum
notes: Lemon Oil, Galbanum, Celluloid, Jasmine, Cashmeran, Dry Amber, Musk
description: Comme des Garcons: Accident

name: Centauri Discovery set
description: Centauri Perfumes honors lost civilizations and cultures by creating fragrances that are inspired by the stars and the movement of the heavens.

name: Elixir
notes: Cognac Essential Oil, Rum Ether, Coffee, Caramel, Texas Cedar, Sandalwood, Patchouli, Musk Accord
description: 4160 Tuesdays

name: Salted Green Mango
notes: Green Mango, Bitter Orange, Petitgrain, Lime, Bergamot, Elemi, Pineapple, Pomelo, Star Gooseberry, Magnolia, Rosa Damascena, Green Chili, Sandalwood, Guava Leaf, Vetiver, Salt Accord, Seaweed
style: Fresh - Clean, Fruity, Salty / Oceanic, Sweet, Tropical / Beachy
description: Salted Green Mango is utterly unique, and dare we say it, one of Strangers Parfumerieâs best. There are green fragrances, there are sour fragrances, there are dry fragrances -  Salted Green Mango manages to be all of the above, but with an otherworldly, exotic flair that defies description. Ostensibly an ode to Bao mango, a green, tart mango native to the  Southern part of Thailand, where perfumer Prin Lomros was born and raised, there is nothing really sweet or juicy about the fruit note here. Instead, the mango is a thing of rare, desiccated beauty - a dark green rind dipped in sea salt that sucks all the moisture out of your mouth only to think of it.

name: SM Cafe
notes: Coffee Absolute, Russian Leather, Suede, Javanol, Lavender, Amber, Benzoin, Tobacco, Cade, Atlas Cedarwood, Tonka Bean, Beeswax Absolute, Maraschino Cherry, Patchouli, Cashmeran, Rose
style: Resinous / Balsamic, Smoky, Spicy
description: Strangers Parfumerie

name: Burning Ben
notes: Hay, Plum, Espresso, Tobacco, Hazelnut, Leather, Patchouli, Incense, Dark Chocolate, Tar, Castoreum, Cognac, Labdanum, Beeswax, Cherry, Amber, Allspice, Javanol, Saffron, Burning Tire
style: Earthy, Gourmand, Resinous / Balsamic, Spicy
description: Burning Ben belongs to Prin Lomrosâ LGBTQ series, and was inspired by a subtle moment in the South Korean movie   "Burningâ where the âBenâ character struggles with a conversation that might be interpreted as homophobic, dredging up a host of complex feelings in himself. Burning Ben is a stunning phenolic scent that similarly unites all sorts of opposing elements â hazelnut, beeswax, burning tires, leather, and forest fruit â creating a surprisingly harmonious and âcompleteâ whole, just like the conflicted humanity of Ben.

name: Cigar Rum
notes: Raisin, Dried Fruits (prune And Cherry), Rum Absolute, Mandarin, Amber, Tobacco, Oakwood, Vetiver, Resins, Labdanum Absolute, Seaweed Absolute
style: Boozy, Gourmand, Smoky, Spicy, Sweet
description: The name - Cigar Rum â is a bit on the nose. But the scent itself? A surprisingly delicate and detailed piece of work that, while definitely redolent of the rich, dried fruit and tobacco leaves soaking in oak caskets of Caribbean island booze implied in the name, has a lightness to it that makes it a fabulously easy wear.

name: Oliver
notes: Bitter Orange, Mojito, Grapefruit, Bergamot, Apricot, Quince, Petitgrain, Mandarin, Fig, Clary Sage, Cypress, Magnolia, Orris Concrete, Basil, Rose De Mai, Orange Flower, Pink Pepper, Coriander, Leather, Tobacco, Ambergris, Patchouli, Vetiver, Ambrette Seed, Oakmoss, Grape Leaves, Grass, Sandalwood, Sea Salt, Linden, Mahogany, Mastic, Olive, Clean Shirt (blue Billowy)
style: Fresh - Citrus, Green / Herbaceous, Woody - Fresh
description: Oliver is an original take on the aromatic herbs and citrus freshness of the traditional eau de cologne. At first, you smell the bright, tart (and always oddly dry) burst of citrus notes of the classic Farina or 4177, with their cleansing and pure aroma designed by nature to awaken and refresh the senses, supported by the crisp, herbal undergrowth of basil leaf, clary sage, and cool, green cypresses.

name: Strangers Discovery Set
description: A captivating and diverse collection from young nose, Prin Lomros, who uses his penchant for cinema to create intriguing fragrances that are as inspired as the films he adores. With such as strong focus on narration, we are certain you'll love each of the stories told by the fragrances in this set.

name: Kira Kira
notes: Mint, Pear, Green Apple, Solar Notes, Hedione, Musk, Sake, Sandalwood, Ambrette, Lily Of The Valley, White Lily, Ambroxan
style: Floral - Fruity, Fruity, Powdery / Soft
description: The heat of summertime in Japan: families gathered in city parks, enjoying a picnic under the brilliant sun which sparkles from the windows of a thousand surrounding skyscrapers in a kaleidoscopic radiance that has a wonderfully onomatopoetic Japanese name:kira kira. Represented here in the form of fragrance, Kira Kira is a delightfully buoyant, exceptionally zen fruity musk scent that weaves Japanese minimalism together with the universal appeal of a day in the summer sun.

name: Comme Un Carrousel
notes: Cola, Rum, Red Wine, Red Apple, Honey, Basil, Rose, Orange Flower, Cinnamon, Coconut, Clove, Tobacco, Vanilla, Myrrh, Amber, Ginger
description: Strangers Parfumerie

style: Floral, Green / Herbaceous, Spicy, Woody - Fresh
description: Strangers Parfumerie

notes: Malted Barley, Hay Absolute, Cypress, Spruce, Labdanum, Peat, Moss, Old Oak Barrel, Cade, Clearwood, Tonka Bean, Smoke, Dried Fruits, Honey, White Chocolate, Licorice, Leather, Orris Butter, Lavender Absolute, Artemisia, Cassia, Atlas Cedar
style: Boozy, Earthy, Smoky, Woody - Dark
description: Strangers Parfumerie

notes: Roasted Coffee, Popcorn, Whisky, Honey, Tobacco, Suede, Vanilla, Hay, Patchouli, Musk, Brandy, Cashmeran, Norlimbanol, Rubber
style: Boozy, Creamy, Earthy, Gourmand, Resinous / Balsamic, Smoky, Sweet
description: Strangers Parfumerie

name: Yuzu Soda
notes: Aldehyde, Camphor, Mint, Yuzu, Bitter Orange, Petitgrain, Pear, Pineapple, Rosemary, Magnolia, Soda, Orris, Basil, Vetiver, Musk, Seaweed
description: Strangers Parfumerie

name: Chokedee
notes: Pandan Leaf, Rice, Jasmine Sambac, Coconut, Neroli, Sandalwood, Cashew Nut, Chommanard, Frangipani, Vetiver, Musk
style: Tropical / Beachy
description: Chokedee is perfumer Prin Lomrosâ take on sweet sticky rice. Itâs a grainy gourmand, both savory and sweet, softly scented with white flowers and toasted coconut. At first, itâs rich and viscous; a chewy, glutinous treat. But with wear it becomes much more nuanced and delicate: a grassy, nutty confection thatâs refined but not uptight.

name: Caffeine Honey
notes: Roasted Coffee, Honey, Tobacco, Hay, Cade, Vanilla, Ambroxan, Patchouli, Popcorn, Musk, Labdanum, Cognac, Amberwood, Almond
style: Gourmand, Sweet
description: Why does coffee make us feel so happy?Why do sweets make people mood better?What if you combine these two elements into one fragrance? Would it help you feel happier? The aroma of roasted black coffee from the coffee roaster wafts around the room. The olfactive note of hay, dried fruit, almond, and tobacco add to the sweetness coming from honey and  caramelized popcorn.

name: Summer Peaches By The Tea Room
notes: Green Tea, Bergamot, Peach, Grass, Orris, Honeysuckle, Freesia, Neroli, Rose, Cedar, Musk, Wisteria
style: Floral - Fruity, Green / Herbaceous, Powdery / Soft
description: Strangers Parfumerie

name: Limonsitano
notes: Limoncello, Mint, Tomato Leaf, Neroli, Seaweed, Thyme, Grapefruit, Bergamot, Rose, Citron, Quince, Pittosporum, Myrtle, Ambrette Seed, Driftwood, Sage, Rosemary, Patchouli, Basil, Green Apple
description: Limonsitano (combining Limoncello and Positano) is a fruity citrus chypre based on the Mediterranean's unique olfactory capture: the landscape of Positano and the Amalfi coast, where the best lemon for Limoncello grows. A place of of salty winds, citrus fruits, and Mediterranean herbs.

name: Sweet Farewell
notes: Coconut Milk, Nipa Palm Leaves, Vanilla, Palm Sugar, Cedar, Rice Powder, Sandalwood, Musk, Pandan Leaves, Tonka Bean, Cashmeran, Ebony
style: Tropical / Beachy
description: Based on the Thai dessert "Kanom Jaak" made from rice powder, palm sugar, coconut milk, coconut flakes and pandan leaf. Grilled on Nipa palm leaves. Kanom is "dessert" in Thai, Jaak can mean Nipa palm leaves, or farewell. Years ago, Kanom Jaak used to be a well known dessert in Thailand. You could find it almost everywhere, but not so much anymore.

name: Cachouli
notes: Cacao Powder, Patchouli, Tonka, Peru Balsam, Milk Note, Orange Flower, Honey, Benzoin, Amber, Mushroom, Soil Accord, Clearwood, Cinnamon, Nutmet, Dried Fruits, Gardenia, Labdanum
style: Gourmand, Sweet
description: Cacao and Patchouliâ hence CachouliâÂ is a perfect combination, where both notes amplify each like in perfect harmony. Cachouli evokes the richness of tropical moist soil. The rich and dry earthiness contrasting with semi-gourmand, tropical white flowers and spices fully exploits its characters of an extraordinary patchouli essential oil.

name: Cherry Amaretto
notes: Amaretto, Brandy, Maraschino Cherry, Sweet Cherry, Cinnamon, Heliotrope, Tonka Bean, Vanilla, Praline, Grenadine, Almond, Peach, Red Apple Juice, Red Wine, Strawberry, White Chocolate
style: Boozy, Gourmand
description: As you have seen before, some of Prin's perfumes are based on cocktails, mocktails, liquor and unique local cuisine. From the raw materials, to Prin's personal experiences and travels, one can see how the odyssey translates to the world of perfumery.

name: A la folie
notes: Amber, Vanilla, Heliotrope, Black Tea, Macarons, Gaiacwood, Tonka Bean, Orange Blossom, Patchouli, Tobacco, CanelÃ©, Rose, Lavender, Hazelnut, Affogato, CrÃ¨me BrÃ»lÃ©e
style: Woody - Amber, Woody Ambery Floral
description: Ã la folie French for into the madness. Wandering in the madness days and nights savoring the aromas of French pastries, desserts and cafe to the mysterious radiance colourful night lights of majestic historic Paris.

name: Mandarava
notes: Bodhi Tree, Borneol, Jasmine Sambac, Himalayan Champaca, Madagascar Ylang Ylang, Indian Marigold, Frankincense, Myrrh, Styrax, Rose, Lotus, Cinnamon, Civet, Nagamortha, Ruh Khus, Ebony, Mysore Sandalwood, Assamese Oud (Hindi), Indonesian Patchouli, Oakmoss, Gardenia, Coriander, Tolu, Siamese Benzoin, Aldehyde, Cumin Seed, Amber, Tuberose, Ash

description: Inspired by the vision of sweet yellow flowers, white elephants, and towering mountain peaks that make up the Thai vision of Trayastrimsa â the Buddhist Nirvana or Afterlife - Mandarava is perfumer Prin Lomrosâ interpretation of what heaven smells like. At first, heaven smells like a lei of heady tropical flowers laid around your neck, the lushness of ripe jasmine, rice pudding-esque champaca, and buttery, banana-rich ylang sending the senses reeling. There is an impression of red fruit swollen with juices, threatening to burst out of the pollen-soaked flowers and run down your arm uninterrupted.

name: Ma Nishtana

style: Fresh - Clean, Smoky, Spicy
description: Ma Nishtana are the first two words in a phrase meaning "Why is tonight different from all other nights?", taken from the stories and lyrics of Had Gadya (the Chava Alberstein version). In a way, it makes sense to turn this philosophical question on its head and ask, âwhy is this incense scent different from all other incense scents?â. After all, incense is a crowded genre and it really takes something special to stand out. Well, Ma Nishtana is something special.

name: Tom Yum

style: Green / Herbaceous, Natural/Organic, Spicy, Unusual / Unexpected
description: Oh boy â just say the word Tom Yum and our mouths water. And the perfume Tom Yum has precisely the same effect. Spritz it on and your senses are instantly afire. Inspired by the famously sweet and sour Tom Yum soup of Thailand, Tom Yum (the scent) belongs to Prissanaâs Thammachatr (Thai for natural) series, meaning that it is all natural â more a recipe than a formula, almost. An extra inducement to lick your own skin when youâre wearing it.

description: Pentimento is a cornucopia of moreish delights, an extravagant still-life that rewards both wearer and audience. Decadent ormolu gilded flower details are backlit by the velveteen drapery shimmer of Hazelnut and Broom. Sumptuously smudged Tonka and Carob bean provide a soft halo-glow against the cool polished stone faÃ§ade of Rum Absolute and the austere crystalline refraction of Tobacco flower. Pentimento is a bouquet that is baroque in its grandeur and is unapologetically rich and indulgent â it is big and encompassing whilst being exceptionally nuanced, faceted, articulate and endlessly complex.

description: PONY BOY: The scent description paints a striking picture: âThe kelpie haunts a Lochside sauna, hissing out a steamed-wood and pink-edged sour lotus haze.â PONY BOY is a sour, fruity-floral-green Extrait de Parfum with fresh, hot-steam elements. There is something both intoxicating and revitalising about it â in a similar vein to one of Jorum Studioâs other popular picks, Paradisi. The name and concept had been in the works for almost a year, but the fragrance itself took longer than average to complete, with tweaks being made daily until the entire team felt it fully captured their vision. The name is inspired by the Kelpie â a shape-shifting folkloric figure said to inhabit lochs in Scottish folklore. âWeâd already touched upon elements of Scottish folklore in HEALING BERRY and FIREWATER, so it was natural to continue along this path. We liked the idea of taking this mythical horse-like figure of temptation and placing it in an unexpected setting,â explained co-founder and perfumer Euan McCall.

It embraces all memories and suggestions coming from that mythical ingredient which has thrilled and excited so many people in the history of perfumery. Unspoken Musk is definitely a symbolic perfume, which conveys the values that I obsessively seek in every perfume: humanity, intimacy from one side, passion and intensity from the other. The perfume is a gentle, dirty yet sweet concoction that will offer comfort to your mind, and embrace you in the most intimate of hugs.â â Francesca Bianchi

This tree, when wounded, produces a resin best known as oud - a precious, mythical material worth more than gold. Obvious Parfums' latest release, Un Oud, highlights this storied sap by emphasizing its resinous and smoky facets with elemi, cedar, and vetiver. Notes of bergamot and cardamom add a cool freshness. The result is a scent that is leathery, woody, and perfectly modern â an obvious choice for an easy-to-wear everyday oud fragrance.

As well, a plethora of very costly floral enfleurages, some of which are: Lilac enfleurage (uber rare), Freesia enfleurage, Bulgarian rose enfleurage, Lily of the Valley enfleurage- uber rare, Gardenia enfleurage and absolute, Pink and Blue lotus enfleurages, Tuberose enfleurage and many many more. These are the Bulgare Fleuri (Bulgarian Floral) portion of the new Ghaliyah, which also has 40% ambergris (Black and White) and musks (Nepali, Kashmiri, and Siberian).

style: Floral - Spicy, Fruity
description: Riding the waves of the sea, challenging sea monsters, the tuberose cried in the rain and dried in the sun. It embraced a block of ambergris and danced with an absolute of red algae. It defended the right to the roots with all his might, clinging to a coral. Its petals, soaked in salt water, were impregnated with purple, obtained by the secretion of a mollusc, one of the few ancient dyes that does not lose intensity with time, but becomes brighter with exposure to sunlight. An indelible pigment, like the intensity of an Extrait de Parfum. With Murice Imperiale I tried to give a scent to the color, called Tyrian red, through notes of rhubarb.

description: Gwen evokes the dizzying power of physical pleasures through its rich arrangement of natural and complex fragrance notes, enveloping the wearer with pure sensuality. The fragrance begins so full of sweet innocence: a subtle citrus and almond combination that first arouses interest and then rapidly transforms into a beautifully defined heart of orchid, sambac jasmine and other head-turning notes. Finally, Gwen opens up in a sea of vanilla, musk, and light woods, as well as benzoin and labdanum.

name: Thumbsucker

description: Thumbsucker was inspired by an ancient Hindu myth about a childless king who got knocked-up when he accidentally drank a magic pregnancy potion meant for his wife. He carried the kid to term, gave birth, and then this high-powered god called Indra stuck his slashed thumb into the newbornâs mouth and fed him with the vitalizing nectar that flowed from his sacred veins. Pretty cool!

name: Mistpouffer
notes: Immortelle, Bergamot, Fig Leaf, Pine, Ozone, Smoke Vetiver, Malt Sugar, Cypriol
description: A Mistpouffer, a.k.a. a skyquake, is an unexplained sound; a strange, inexplicable rumbling or crash. In Japan the phenomenon is called uminari or âcries from the sea.â In Connecticut, Native Americans called the area around Mount Tom Machimoodus, meaning âPlace of Bad Noises.â Possible explanations: solar winds, shallow earthquakes, extraterrestrial shenanigans, subsurface civilizations. Early settlers in North America were told by the native Iroquois that the booms were the sound of The Great Spirit shaping the earth.

name: Moonmilk
description: In 1555, the Swiss naturalist Conrad Gessner went caving in the Alps and discovered Moonmilk - the creamy pearlescent substance that leaks from limestone and dolomite. Moved by its beauty, he wrote, âAt night, the moon shines into the cave so brightly the light itself seems to take form like a mist. It gathers in the ceiling and makes the limestone stalactites glitter and gleam, dripping tears of white liquid into puddles on the rock floor.â

name: Fantome de Maules
notes: Bergarmot, Galbanum, Green Leaves, Lemon, Black Pepper, Cardamom, Cashmeran, Coriander, Forest Flowers, Lavender Absolute, Ambroxan, Cedarwoods, Habanolide, Labdanum, Oakmoss, Sandalwood, Tonka Bean, Vetiver
description: âWho is FantÃ´me de Maules?â asks Stora Skuggan, the mysterious Swedish fragrance house whose name translates to âBig Shadow.â Good question. Maules, a French-speaking village in Switzerland, is purportedly home to a fantÃ´me â a benevolent loner/spirit/survivalist who haunts the local woodlands â picking flowers, cracking twigs, rustling leaves and cloaking the forest in a phantasmal veil of eeriness and intrigue.

name: Silphium

style: Floral - Ambery Spice, Floral - Spicy, Spicy
description: Silphium is an extinct plant that was used in the classical era as a spice, a medicinal herb, a perfume ingredient, and as an aphrodisiac. It was highly prized but would only grow wild on the North African Coast, making it hard to get and extremely valuable. The demand for silphium eventually became so overwhelming that it was harvested to extinction, and the taste and smell of the spice was regretfully lost over time.

Along the route stood one single acacia tree, the only living thing in an ocean of sand. This was the most isolated tree in the world, 100 miles from any other.

description: The original Chronic stood out for its dynamic version of a classic amber- a spicy, creamy, tantalizing modern gourmand. For the Extreme Red edition, Byron knew better than to just reconfigure an already perfect thing. But The Chronic Extreme Red is a brand new take on a spicy amber gourmand, one that ups the ante by balancing the warmth of the original with an impossibly bright, fresh, mouthwatering collection of fruit- juicy melon and pear alongside tart raspberry. And just to turn up the seduction factor, everything is blanketed in a layer of sinfully indulgent whipped cream. Lovers of sophisticated fruity vanilla gourmands like La Danza Delle Libellule and Lira, don't miss this stunning new addition.

notes: Peach, Caramel, Strawberry, Raspberry, Pink Berries, Ginger, Labdanum, Amber, Vanilla, Patchouli, Musk, Oud From Laos
style: Boozy, Creamy, Fruity, Gourmand, Sweet
description: Byron Parfums

name: Black Dragon
notes: Honey, Pineapple, Mango, Cigar, Bourbon Vanilla, Sandalwood Base, Patchouli, Amber, Musk
description: Byron Parfums

name: Musc Panache
notes: Tamarind, Mandarin, Bitter Orange, Bergamot, Cardamom, Iris, Honeysuckle, Vetiver, Patchouli, Cedar, Tonka Bean, Musks, Amber
style: Fresh - Citrus
description: Maurice Roucel wanted Musc Panache to be the antidote to the pungency of natural musk, or even the sultry thickness of his Musc Ravageur (for Frederic Malle). And wow, has he succeeded! Musc Panache is fresh and sparkling from top to toe, with a glass-like shine that almost seems to refract light. The musk here, though clearly musk, is unusually transparent.

name: Rose Rebatchi
notes: Pink Pepper, Saffron, Raspberry, Peach, Apple, Damask Rose, Turkish Rose, Iris Butter, White Musks, Sandalwood, Ambergris, Leather
style: Floral, Floral - Fruity, Floral - Spicy, Spicy
description: Rose Rebatchi, a rose soliflore, that will make you feel like you are immersed in a personal scent cloud of powdered rose petals. The Bulgarian rose absolute used in the topnotes lends a wet, crisp lemony greenness that calls to mind a rose freshly plucked from its thorny stem, still dripping with morning dew. In time, this crystalline rose turns richer and jammier, saffron working its âred goldâ magic with Turkish rose absolute to produce a deeply velvety texture that nonetheless manages, thanks to a powdery iris, to remain sheer and elegant on the skin.

name: Feu Patchouli
notes: Sweet Orange, Citron, Bergamot, Pink Pepper, Black Pepper, Blackcurrant, Aldehydes, Clove, Cinnamon, Pink Carnation, Davana, Patchouli, Myrrh, Frankincense, Labdanum, Vanilla, Musks, Driftwood, Caramel, Oakmoss
style: Earthy, Fresh - Citrus, Resinous / Balsamic, Spicy, Woody - Spicy
description: With Feu Patchouli, perfumer Bertrand Duchaufour and brand art director Mohamed Rebatchi, decided to completely reinvent the chypre structure by taking patchouli from where it usually lurks, in the basenotes, and inverting it, so that the patchouli commands center stage from top to bottom. This has the effect of infusing the entire scent with a dry but velvety darkness that sometimes reads as moss, sometimes as earth, but mostly as deeply-spiced woods.

name: Joyeux Osmanthe
notes: Nectarine, Apricot, Cinnamon, Green Leaves, Osmanthus, Tuberose, Orange Blossom, Honeysuckle, Jasmine, Rose, Iris, Musk, Cashmeran, Cedar, Amber
style: Floral - Fruity, Floral - White, Fruity
description: Joyful indeed! Osmanthus absolute has a very sophisticated apricot-skin suede aroma that, alone, can seem rather discreet. By choosing to pair it with a luxuriantly rich, creamy tuberose, and a rustle of crisp, leafy greenery, Maurice Roucel has put some color back into its cheeks, imbuing it with the healthy vigor of a jungle vine. Osmanthus pulls its own weight, of course, the leathery elegance of the material restraining the peachy, creamy-mentholated tuberose until it emerges smelling modern and well-behaved (for a tuberose).

name: Cuir Tassili
notes: Black Pepper, Elemi, Lemon, Sage, Iris Concrete, Tea Leaf, Myrrh, Leather, Patchouli
style: Earthy, Floral - Ambery Spice, Floral - Spicy, Resinous / Balsamic, Smoky, Spicy
description: Cuir Tassili is less a fragrance than it is an entire scentscape, capturing the broad sweep of aromas gathered from the Tassili desert, in south east Algeria, home to the nomadic pastoralists known as the Tuareg Aijer. The lemon and iris in the topnotes conjure up the acidic tang of the powdery lemon sherbets the Tuareg people ingest to cool themselves after a long, hot day out in the desert, while tea, myrrh, and black pepper notes summon the thick swirls of smoke and tannins of the black tea they brew under cloth tents.

name: Maison Rebatchi - Discovery Set
description: Immerse yourself in the world of Maison Rebatchi. This discovery set includes 8 x 2ml atomizer sprays featuring fragrance creations from the world's most renowned perfumers: Bertrand Duchaufour, Maurice Roucel, Karine Chevallier, Randa Hammami, AliÃ©nor Massenet.

name: Jasmin Satin
notes: Gardenia, Palisander Rosewood, Orange Blossom, Jasmine Sambac, Lily, Peach, Sand, Virginia Cedar, Amber, Musk
style: Floral - White, Powdery / Soft, Resinous / Balsamic
description: Mohamed Rebatchi, the son of Algerian immigrants, wanted Jasmin Satin to replicate the glorious smell of jasmine drifting through the night breeze he smelled while riding his bike through jasmine fields one evening in his native land. And indeed, this scent captures the heady aroma of white flowers as smelled in nature, as one passes by vine-covered walls and secret gardens in warm, Southern cities.

name: Vanille Riviera
notes: Bergamot, Galbanum, Jasmine Absolute, Ylang-ylang, Cedarwood, Vanilla Planifolia Absolute
description: Vanille Riviera draws on the memory of a floral and green vanilla, freshly harvested in Madagascar. Lightened by vegetal tones, the dark and carnal side of the pod meets the brightness of jasmine. Some woody and spicy shades highlight the sensuality of this bewitching melody.

name: Unique'e Luxury - Discovery Set
description: Uniqueâe Luxury Discovery Set - 14 x 2ml atomizer extrait de parfumes, include:

name: Hidden Accords
notes: Pink Pepper, Orange Blossom, Pear, Coffee, Jasmine, Vanilla, Patchouli, Cedar Wood
style: Creamy, Earthy, Floral - Fruity, Gourmand, Spicy, Sweet
description: Lavish, elegant, and glittering outside and in, each Unique'e scent is like a perfectly packaged gift- discovering its secrets is half the fun. And Hidden Accords, in particular, is a luxurious discovery- an alluring blend of gourmand, floral, and spice notes that intrigues with its complexity even as it nearly overwhelms us with sheer, indulgent pleasure.

name: Deluxe Travel Set - 14 x 11ml
description: For perfume lovers who want to experience the complete collection from Unique'e Luxury with a deluxe, travel spray set.

name: Mashumaro
notes: Marshmallow, Cashmere Wood, Milk, Incense, Caramel, Musk, Muguet, Rose, Praline, Vanilla
style: Gourmand, Sweet, Woody - Amber
description: You could hear the name Mashumaro and assume that the newest scent from Unique'e Luxury is a frivolous, lighthearted confection, but that's a mistake that your first sniff of this potent stunner will quickly clear up. Mashumaro is, in fact, a beast of a gourmand- warm, rich, and sweet, with more than enough woodsy, masculine DNA lurking beneath the surface to place it firmly in the pantheon of signature scents that leave an long-lasting trail deep into the night. Sensually smoky vibes start with cashmere wood, followed by mouthwatering caramel, praline and vanilla that leap to life off the skin. Finally, the blend calls musk, muguet, and rose and gets even warmer and cozier. Woody, rich, and powerfully sexy, Mashumaro is a delectable and unforgettable treat.

name: Mangonifiscent
notes: Mango, Bergamot, Geranium, Rose, Jasmine, Sandalwood, Patchouli, Vanilla, Honey, Amber, Musk, And Mossy Notes
style: Fruity, Tropical / Beachy
description: Unique'e Luxury

name: Al-Arabiya
notes: Saffron, Coffee, Henna, Jasmine. Bakhoor, Frankincense, Oud.
description: Inspired by the sights and smells experienced in old Arabian souks, Al Arabiya features a multi-layered ensemble of scent notes that is sure to enchant your senses. Spray some on your wrist... inhale... close your eyes. Youâll find yourself at an old attar shop in which the aromas of frankincense and bakhoor infuse every surface - even the walls emit their sacred scents. Countless gold-trimmed glass flacons ornament beckoning shelves, their sweet, floral and spicy notes vying for attention. An incense burner sitting on the vendor's table emits a plume of scented smoke. Oud, frankincense, bakhoor. Their beauty is hypnotic.

name: Blu
description: Blu is a one of those Agar Aura perfumes that doesn't fall under a particular category or genre. Its not Arabian, French, or Japanese. And yet, the way it comes together, it forms a seamless fragrance that smells simply incredible, and utterly unique.

name: Layali
description: The oud-rose combo is just about the most common pair used in perfumes that contain oud.  But when it comes to western 'oud' perfumes, virtually all of them use synthetic oud. In Arabian oud-based mukhallats, real oud might be used but as a general rule, the lowest grades of oud are used. Then there's the addition of synthetic musks, Iso E Super, and other aromachemicals - which is standard, in both traditions.

name: Agar Attar
description: Agar Attar is what 'oud perfume' ought to smell like.

name: Java
description: Java is successor to our Hutan Kalimantan, an attar that was so popular that it was sold out almost instantly.

name: Le Cheikh Boheme
notes: Oud, Coumarin, Oakmoss, Labdanum, Leather, Frankincense, Spices, Amber, Rose Otto, Mimosa Extract, Citrus
style: Fougere, Woody - Dark
description: A marriage between Eastern and Western perfumery, Le Cheikh BohÃ¨me is the spray version of Agar Aura's popular oil parfum âIntiqaâieâ â Arabic for âeclecticâ â a fusion of French perfumery with an utterly traditional Arabian core. This is Intiqaâie in spray format, but a strong all-natural coumarin tincture base takes it to a whole new level.

name: Malinau
description: After the immense success of 'agarwood soliflore' perfumes like Kinam Attar and Kelantan, we are excited to unveil the next installment in the series, Malinau. Together with Kinam (Kyara), agarwood from the Malinau region of Borneo island ranks as the most appealing of the all the agarwood varieties. And not just any agarwood from Malinau, but specifically Aquilaria Microcarpa species wood from the Mentarang district.

name: Oud Fougere
description: If the ambergris doesn't hit you over the head, the oud certainly will. Although the FougÃ¨re genre marked the beginning of the widespread use of synthetics in perfumery with the ingredient coumarin, our Oud FougÃ¨re was crafted using solely nature-derived ingredients.

name: Yellow Kinam
description: For the *PERFECT* yellow kinam aroma, I would need agarwood from Hainan. And I insist on distilling only wild agarwood. The scarcity, cost, and logistics rendered an in-house wild Hainanese agarwood distillation it unfeasible.

name: Bushi no Kaori
description: In medieval Japan, among the most avid users of agarwood were the Samurai. They would scent their body armor and helmets with the smoke of agarwood before heading into battle. Bushi No Kaori is an agarwood-intensive blend created to mimic what a Samurai warrior's quarters would have smelled like just before he headed out for battle.

name: Bois Datchai
notes: Blackcurrant, Black Tea, Cinnamon, Patchouli, Cedar, Leather
style: Fruity, Smoky, Spicy, Woody - Spicy
description: Bois DatchaÃ¯ is a nostalgic delight! The pop of juicy, mentholated blackberries against a backdrop of soft, smoky woods, incense, and rubbery black tea, all warmly spiced with cinnamon, puts us immediately in a Fall frame of mind. Created to summon the atmosphere of a Russian dacha in the middle of the woods, Bois DatchaÃ¯ indeed does tease the senses with the suggestion of roaring fires, mulled wine, and blackberry picking in the woods with oneâs grandparents. It smells innocent, a big fruity-woody experience simply designed to invoke joy.

name: Santal Volcanique
notes: Ginger, Cardamom, Limette, Sandalwood, Cedar, Ylang Ylang, Coffee, Musks
style: Earthy, Powdery / Soft, Spicy, Woody - Spicy
description: Santal Volcanique is a thoroughly modern take on the spicy, musky fougÃ¨re genre once populated by the likes of Gucci Envy and By (Dolce Gabbana), but now largely overridden by new tastes in masculine perfumery, which run much more towards sweet and ambery. Santal Volcanique is, however, a glorious return to form for the genre, and if anything is capable of making these rugged, manly woods-and-spice affairs the (old) new standard for masculine perfumery, then it is this.

name: Rose Saltifolia
notes: Blood Orange, Pink Pepper, Rose Centifolia, Seaweed, Cashmeran
style: Floral, Fresh - Aquatic, Green / Herbaceous, Salty / Oceanic, Woody - Fresh
description: Rose SaltifOlia â a portmanteau of salt and rose centifolia â is the perfect name for this scent. At its center glows a pink rose of the old-fashioned cabbage variety, hundreds of petals deep and separated only by the sparkle of dew drops. But this rose is tougher than she seems. An English cottage rose, yes, but one that has shot up out of a tangle of seagrass on the salt flats of a Brittany beach. The salinity of the breezes stiffens the rose petals, giving the scent a prismatic âfleur de selâ dimension, whereby one moment you smell only the virginal softness of the rose, and the next, a floral salt dotted with dried rose petals, seagrass, and other small, hardy marine flowers.

name: Absinthe Boreale
notes: Juniper, Eucalyptus, Mint, Lemon, Lavender, Artemisia, Musks
style: Green / Herbaceous, Powdery / Soft
description: The icy brush of herbs in the opening of Absinthe BorÃ©ale sends a pleasurable shiver down oneâs spine, painting a watercolor of a pale Northern European sun illuminating the lacy stalks of lavender, rosemary, and artemisia that have been caught in the first frost of the season. Silvery mint, oakmoss, and eucalyptus thrum from the background, keeping the real star of this scent â lavender â on ice. Indeed, Absinthe BorÃ©ale smells like you imagine it might taste. Part cocktail, part Medieval elixir, full of strange herbs and aromatics that are familiar individually but exotic when combined.

name: Papyrus Moleculaire
notes: Coriander, Papyrus, Sandalwood, Black Pepper, Tonka, Tobacco, Leather
style: Earthy, Powdery / Soft, Spicy, Woody - Spicy
description: Papyrus MolÃ©culaire is addictive stuff. Transparent layers of clean, spicy wood and peppery papyrus are laid down one on top of the other until a soft green leather takes shape, an alchemy that makes you stop and marvel. The luminescence of the woods and leather is aptly captured in the word MolÃ©culaire, as even the scentâs heavier, more resinous materials, like frankincense, elemi, and tonka bean, appear suspended in molecules with plenty of air and space between, like the best fairy lights ever.

name: Iris Malikhan
notes: Cypress, Lentisque, Galbanum, Orris Butter, Mimosa, Vanilla, Leather, Amber, Musks
style: Creamy, Floral, Gourmand, Green / Herbaceous, Powdery / Soft
description: This is an iris fragrance that takes what you think about the noble rhizome and turns it on its head. Powdery or rooty? Thatâs the question asked of all iris fragrances. Iris MalikhÃ¢n not only gives you both but feeds them to you in surprising ways. Galbanum resin and lentisque give the earthy orris a thrillingly green, bitter facet that elevates it from soil-encrusted root to sunburnt garrigue. A touch of blackcurrant leaf adds to the resiny, woody dryness, joining with cypress and a supple leather note to form an impression of the interior of a Florentine leather craftsmanâs atelier that has just taken possession of a pile of lambskins. This is sturdy enough to remind you that leather is the hide of an animal, after all, but not so rough that it stains the glowing purity of the orris.

name: Lys Solaberg
notes: Lily, Calamus, Gaiac, Ambroxan, Oakmoss, Cedar, Oak Chips, Tobacco, Cistus Labdanum
style: Earthy, Floral - White, Green / Herbaceous, Resinous / Balsamic, Woody - Dark
description: Lys SÃ¸laberg is a lily that surprises. Instead of emphasizing the flowerâs greenness or its savory, almost meaty character, the perfumer has chosen instead to highlight the flowerâs sultry, heady facet. This then is lily on her way home from a night out with friends. Swaying a little, all the good Cognac and dark fruitcake and double cream she has ingested threatens to spill over onto her pearlescent flowers. This is an unfamiliar picture of the normally pristine lily, and we absolutely love it.

name: Hibiscus Mahajad
notes: Rose Damascena, Vanilla, Leather, Spearmint, Ambrette, Cassis, Cinnamon
style: Creamy, Floral, Floral - Spicy, Resinous / Balsamic, Spicy
description: Hibiscus MahajÃ¡d captures an olfactory revelation experienced by Maison Crivelli founder Thibaud Crivelli while drinking a cup of hibiscus tea in the heart of a gemstone market. Known as bissap (in Senegal) or karkadeh (in the Maghreb and Levant), tea made from dried hibiscus petals is as popular for its raspberry pink color as for its fruity flavor, but we find it also has a very grown up resinous vanilla or floral aftertaste in the mouth. Quentin Bisch cleverly accentuates this rosy, vanillic facet by draping the finest rose and vanilla absolutes produced under Givaudanâs prestigious ORPUR label over the bow of the bright, rhubarb-like stalkiness of the hibiscus tea.

name: Experiences Olfactives - Maison Crivelli Discovery Set
description: Sensorial explorations and unexpected adventures await with the innovative discovery selections from Maison Crivelli.  Each creation has its own inspiration and a very different olfactory signature from the other scents, making this an original present in which each fragrance is a surprise in itself.

style: Floral - Ambery Spice, Powdery / Soft
description: Created in collaboration with the designer Violette Hay, this fragrance is a modernized ode to the violet flower. While it does contain hints of parma violet, itâs main focus is a hay-like suede note, with apricot undertones. This is not a sweet fragrance and itâs velvety texture stays fairly close to the skin. Soft, pixie-like, natural, golden.

name: Petrichor
notes: Damp Earth, Lemon, Immortelle, Orris, Vetiver, Ambergris, Sandalwood, Moss, Musk
description: Petrichor is an ode to orris, but not in the typical fashion. Itâs reminiscent of cold, wet dirt, while at the same time carrying a specific luminescence. There is a rooty and shimmering effect, undeniably like the scent of fresh rain on dirt and cold stone. Conceptual, cold, dry, powdery, dramatic and avant-garde.

name: The Sun Card
notes: Heliotrope, Sweet Orange, Hay, Frangipani, Sheer Amber
description: Pull back the silk curtains and the smell of frangipani flowers strewn about on the tarot readerâs table invites you in. As she reveals cards, scent memories of fresh hay, amber and sweet orange come to the fore. Heliotrope and spices perfume the air as she pulls the final card, the Sun. She tells you itâs an omen of vitality, confidence, and abundance: the sunâs gift is a joyous celebration. Ceremonial, regal, fresh.

name: La Divina
notes: Rose, Cassis, Poplar Buds, Velvet Musk, Oakmoss, Sandalwood

description: âMaggie the Cat is Alive! Iâm Alive! Why are you afraid of the truth?!â - Elizabeth Taylor in Tennessee Williamsâ âCat on a Hot Tin Roofâ dir. Richard Brook, 1958.

notes: Pomegranate, Saffron, Clove, Nutmeg, Cinnamon, Coffee Absolute, Champaca, Rose, Leather, Tobacco, Tonka Bean, Nagarmotha, Oud (Thailand), Oud (India), Patchouli, Amber, Incense, Vanilla

description: Azman

name: Two Minutes After The Kiss
notes: Turkish Rose, Pepper, Frankincense, Cardamom, Oud, Patchouli, Myrrh, Beeswax, Selectone, Sandalwood, Vetiver, Leather Accord, Labdanum
style: Woody - Dark, Woody - Spicy
description: Mysteries are typically dark. Our mystery of 2 Minutes After the Kiss is one thatâs full of light, yet its unusual take on sunny florals as it heads to a woody heart defies the obvious.The journey starts off with a rose-centric intro surrounded by hints of white florals and a good measure of silver frankincense. The peppery motifs of Jamaican pepper add an invigorating touch before the scents segues into a lush, sensual heart.Passion swells the heart, making it more natural and richer with the patchouly that introduces a generous measure of the lush Laos Oud. The translucent rose blushes under the balsamic smoke of the luxurious Oud. Caressed by creamy sandalwood, the base becomes softer and even more mysterious.

name: Lait de Biscuit
notes: Gourmand Notes, Caramel, Vanilla
style: Gourmand, Sweet
description: Chabaud

name: Lait Concentre
notes: Milk, Coconut, Caramel
style: Gourmand, Sweet
description: âLait ConcentrÃ©â evokes a childhood memory that is connected to the popular and delicious condensed milk. It is fairylike, suggesting a transparent veil, merging smoothly with skin, like a fleeting memory that you can just hold on to with your fingertips - a delicious and ethereal eau de toilette.At the beginning of the fragrance we encounter bright milk notes, that remind us of the delicious concentrated milk that the French confectioner Nicolas Appert invented in the 19th Century. The light and seemingly transparent fragrance continues with a slightly tart, unsweetened coconut and closes with restrained caramel notes.

name: Lait de Vanille

style: Gourmand, Sweet

name: Wam

name: Eau Coeur
