style: Creamy
description: Eau Coeur is an intense encounter with blooming splendour. The magnificent, alluring and graceful moment, simultaneously tender yet evocative in its delicateness. In its wake a blend of lingering sensual tenderness and infinite, passionate seduction. Eau Coeur is a radiant fragrance, straight from the heart, desiring love.

name: Grand Beau
notes: Pine Absolue, Juniper Berry, Angelica Root, Tuberose Absolue, Olibanum, Vetiver, Ambrette, Seaweed Absolue, Ambrocenide
description: Grand Beau is a French phrase that describes both a weekend under a blue sky, and that beautiful person we would like to be; mature in spirit and elegant in appearance.

name: French Flower
notes: Pear, Nigerian Ginger, Tuberose, Orange Blossom, Chinese Tea, Ambroxan
style: Floral - White
description: French Flower takes you to the headspace that is thousands of French tuberose flowers in a field at night, the lingering warmth of the sun coaxing the flowers to relax and release their heady, intoxicating scent in one long sigh. Featuring both tuberose absolutes and an enfleurage of tuberose from flowers grown organically by MatiÃ¨re Premiere itself, French Flower puts its money when its mouth is. This is full-figured, lush tuberose perfume that doesnât stint on the quality or quantity of tuberose.

name: Falcon Leather
notes: Saffron, Leather, Birch Tar, Ciste Labdanum, Benzoin Absolute
style: Masculine
description: If the MatiÃ¨re Premiere collection was a menagerie, Falcon Leather would be the American bald-headed eagle, bristling with the raw masculine energy of a practiced predator. Opening with a gut punch of smoky saffron, which twists everything into the shape of a leather harness, Falcon Leather feels immediately powerful, like sinew shifting over bone.

name: Parisian Musc
notes: Virginian Cedar, Ambrette (musk Mallow), Fig Leaf, Musk, Ambrettolide, Ambroxan
description: Parisian Musc espouses the philosophy of Aurelien Guichardâs MatiÃ¨re Premiere to perfection by carving away all the extraneous noise so that the simple beauty of its main raw material â here ambrette seed â can shine. And shine it does. Ambrette seed is one of natureâs marvels. Wholly vegetal in origin, it replicates the soft, fuzzy comfort of a cashmere sweater on clean, perfumed skin. In Parisian Musc, all of the uniquely fruity, anisic qualities of ambrette seed come through in a milky cloud of scent that blooms immediately upon contact with skin. It smells alive and intimate, yet translucent and delicately green.

name: Neroli Oranger
notes: Neroli, Bergamot, Tunisian Orange Blossom, Musk, Ylang-ylang
description: Quite simply the freshest, loveliest combination of sweet, honeyed-citrusy orange blossom and bitter green neroli on the market. No need to complicate proceedings or reinvent the wheel when you have two organic raw materials as exquisite as the Tunisian orange blossom and French neroli used here. A touch of buttery ylang to emphasize the fruity and floral nuances of the orange blossom, a light application of woody white musks to sustain the soapy sharpness of the neroli, and a hit of limey-green bergamot for zest and freshness â that is all.

name: Radical Rose
notes: Rose, Pepper, Saffron, Patchouli, Labdanum
style: Floral - Fruity
description: The only rose fragrance in the world to use Rosa Centifolia oil from the perfumerâs own organic farm (in France), this is the rose fragrance that you will smell that makes you realize that there is a Radical Rose-shaped hole in your fragrance collection. This is a rose that transverses the entire rose spectrum from peppery and bright (in the beginning) to spicy and earthy (in the heart) to mossy and ambery (in the basenotes), circling briefly through rose lokhoum, rosewater, soap, and wood stations along the way. It is, quite frankly, a rose tour de force.

name: Santal Austral
notes: Iris, Sandalwood, Tonka Bean, Benzoin
style: Earthy, Floral, Fresh, Woody - Fresh
description: Australian sandalwood â santalum spiccatum â possesses a tart, woody freshness that is as mentally uplifting as walking through a forest of freshly-felled timber. In Santal Austral, this facet is cleverly emphasized by an iris topnote that is as silvery as the sandalwood. Together, these notes produce an effect that is both dusty and sparkling, for a sandalwood champagne of sorts.

name: Encens Suave
notes: Coffee, Incense, Madagascar Vanilla, Benzoin, Labdanum
description: Wow! Why did it take so long for someone to cross the genres of church incense and gourmand? Whatever the answer, we sure are grateful that Aurelien Guichard took that leap of faith with Encens Suave in 2019. In the opening, the divine aroma of frankincense bubbling on a censer mingles with the fragrant scent of roasting coffee bean to produce an effect that is both smoky and bite-your-arm-off edible. The warm ginger- or cardamom-like tones of the coffee accentuate the spicy Coca Cola notes of the Somalian frankincense used here. It is a match made in incense heaven.

name: Crystal Saffron
notes: Musk Habanolide, Saffron, Ambroxan, Incense
style: Sweet, Woody - Amber
description: Built around one of the highest quality saffron oils in the world â from the Kozani region of Greece â Crystal Saffron is a 360 degree screen capture of this most expensive and complex of spices, from its piercing iodine flare to its dusky, sunburnt leather finish. Habanolide musk accentuates the delicate glow of the saffron until it shines as brightly as freshly-polished gold, while Somalian incense creates a woody-smoky resinousness that acts as a dark backdrop against which the bright, almost foiled properties of that amazing saffron can shine.

name: Colonia Nobile
notes: Bergamot, Petit Grain, Neroli, Karismal, Jasmine, Benzoin, Ambrettolide, Ambroxan, Oak Moss
description: While Colonia Nobile does indeed evoke the timeless elegance of a gentleman's eau de cologne, it is, in fact, a fully unisex eau de parfum- charmingly vintage to be certain, yet uniquely sophisticated. A classic composition based on vibrant bergamot, lemon, and petitgrain is balanced against warm, resinous amber. The typical freshness of a cologne is further enhanced by a floral bouquet, enhanced with the velvety muskiness of natural ambrette seed and rich oakmoss- both a perfect EDC but also something else entirely. For those who appreciate a romantic twist on an all-time favorite, Colonia Nobile is a perfect selection.

description: Esco Pazzo was an exercise in patience and perseverance. Lorenzo Pazzaglia pictured the fragrance perfectly in his mindâs eye, but creating it was a whole other thing. He stuck at it, somewhat doggedly, refusing to give up. And he did it: the idea became a reality. The scintillating opening of lemon, cedar, bergamot and grapefruit, together with pepper, creates a brilliant burst of freshness, ushering in a spicy aromatic heart. The dry down reveals a velvety base of leather, tobacco and amber.
